meaning: n.鏈夊崼鏄熷煄鐨勫ぇ閮藉競
examples: This new link virtually makes Malm and Copenhagen a single conurbation, [making] commuting from one city to another even easier.
Take Middlesbrough, a city in the northern Teesside conurbation.
Yes, it would be interesting to see how these phone patterns tie up with existing urban conurbation patterns.
The government will also allow local authorities who work together to have their own "conurbation mayor" should they want one.
"The costs in terms of traffic delays as a result of piecemeal maintenance will be badly felt by road users in the south-east Dorset conurbation, " he added.
